(Kakamigahara)}} [[JA:御井神社 (各務原市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11488771}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Mii Shrine | image = [[File:Mii jinja Kakamigahara2008-2.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kakamigahara|Q641491|WD=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}} Mii-cho 5-8 | deity = {{ill|lt=Kimata-no-kami|Q11092833|WD=Q11092833|lt=Mii-no-kami|1=Kimata-no-Kami}} | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|23|23.35|N|136|50|15.33|E|display=title,inline}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
{{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = Third Saturday/Sunday of October | map = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|lat_deg=35|lat_min=23|lat_sec=23.3|lon_deg=136|lon_min=50|lon_sec=15.3|label=Mii Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|23|23.35|N|136|50|15.33|E}}}} }} '''Mii Shrine''' (みいじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara|Q641491|WD=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}} (former Kakamigun, Mino Province). === Overview === It is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. Mii-no-kami is worshiped as a deity of trees, water, and safe childbirth. The area is rich in groundwater, and Kakamigahara's water-source facilities are nearby. Historically, {{ill|lt=Mount Mii|Q11354265|WD=Q11354265|1=Mount Mii}} itself was considered part of the shrine precinct. Originally the local tutelary shrine of Mii Village (Saraki-mura), it is now regarded as the tutelary shrine of the Inaba area of Kakamigahara. It is one of the city's five {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} shrines. === History === The founding date is unknown. Tradition says the original shrine stood on the south slope of Mt. Mii and later moved to the summit. In the Sengoku period, {{ill|lt=Mii Castle|Q11354255|WD=Q11354255|1=Miiyama Castle}} was built on the mountain by Toki-affiliated forces, and the shrine was moved to its current otabisho-area location at the foot of the mountain. A portion of the shrine was also divided and became Tenjin Shrine in Kaminakaya. Mii Castle fell in 1548 during conflict between the Toki and Saito sides and was abandoned. The current honden, noritoden, and haiden were completed in 1941.Shikinaikinpei Mii Jinja guide booklet. On December 18, 1952, it was designated a Kinpeisha by the Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines.{{sfn|木村|1997|p=73}} A ritual called ''sakaura shinji'' is held on the first Sunday of April: sake is poured into a bamboo segment with a small hole, sealed with paper, purified, and cast into a pond; if it sinks, the year is expected to be rainy and abundant, and if it floats, drought and poor harvest are expected. === Enshrined deities === === Main deity === * {{ill|lt=Kimata-no-kami|Q11092833|WD=Q11092833|lt=Mii-no-kami|1=Kimata-no-Kami}} === Goshi (merged deities) === * {{ill|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi|Q478504|WD=Q478504|WD=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-kami|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-kami|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} === Subsidiary shrines === * Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}) * Mikuwa Shrine ({{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}}) * Akiba Shrine ({{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Kagutsuchi-no-kami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}}) * Sumiyoshi Shrine ({{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}) === Other monuments === * {{ill|lt=Chukonhi|Q11274518|WD=Q11274518|lt=Russo-Japanese War memorial|1=monument to the loyal dead}} (1909) * Statue of {{ill|lt=Nogi Maresuke|Q468122|WD=Q468122|1=Nogi Maresuke}} (1936) * Statue of {{ill|lt=Ninomiya Sontoku|Q968964|WD=Q968964|1=Ninomiya Sontoku}} === Major rituals === * Jan 1: Saitansai / New Year's festival * Feb 10: Mikuwa festival * Old lunar Feb 14: Akiba festival * Third Sunday of Feb: Kinen-sai * Mar 15: Sumiyoshi festival * First Saturday of Apr: Okunomiya festival and Suijin festival * First Sunday of Apr: Chinka-sai * Jun 30: Summer purification * Oct (second Sunday): Grand festival * Nov (fourth Sunday): Niiname-sai * Dec 30: Year-end purification * Dec 31: Joya festival * Monthly: 1st day and 15th day rites === Cultural properties === ; Kakamigahara City designated cultural properties * Komainu of Mii Shrine (one pair){{Cite web|ja|date=2024-08-20|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005178.html|title=City designated cultural properties|publisher=Kakamigahara City|accessdate=2013-04-25}} * Archaeological materials excavated from Mii Kofun (3 items) === Location === * {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kakamigahara|Q641491|WD=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}}{{ill|lt=Mii-cho (Kakamigahara)|Q131870038|WD=Q131870038|lt=Mii-cho|1=Mii-cho}} 5-8 === Transport === * Kakamigahara Fureai Bus (Inaba line), get off at "Mii-cho Kominkan-mae," about 3 minutes on foot. === Okunomiya === [[File:Mii Shrine at Mount Mii 2010-12-19.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Okunomiya of Mii Shrine near the summit of Mt. Mii]] Near the summit of Mt. Mii, where the former shrine once stood. It was divided from Mii Shrine after the fall of Mii Castle (date unknown). Mt. Mii also has ritual sites associated with iwakura worship and many excavated artifacts, as well as a kofun (Miiyama Kofun). === Deity === * Mii-no-kami === Address === * Government land without lot number, Naka, Kakamigahara (near summit of Mt. Mii) === Miiike Ryujin Shrine === A separate shrine of Mii Shrine, also called "Mii Shrine Ikenomiya" or "Miiike Ryujin Shrine." Mii Pond at the foot of Mt. Mii is said to have been dug by Mii-no-kami and is an oxbow-lake remnant of the old Kiso River channel. In 1929, during landfill work around Mii Pond, a worker operating an irrigation pump reportedly entered a trance and moved like a dragon crawling on the ground; after this and related experiences, a small shrine was established by enshrining the spirit of Mii Shrine together with a dragon deity.{{Sfn|前渡歴史保存会|2024|p=39}} The present building was reconstructed in 1976 using part of old materials. === Deity === * Dragon deity (Miiike Ryujin / Miike Ryujin) === Precinct shrines === * Suijin * Kappa shrine === Address === * {{ill|lt=Miiyamacho|Q133214736|WD=Q133214736|1=Miiyamacho}}, Kakamigahara (about 15 minutes on foot from Mii Shrine) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book |ja |editor=Maedo History Preservation Society |title=Shrines and Temples: Kakamigahara Inaba District Edition |publisher=Maedo History Preservation Society |date=2024 |ref={{SfnRef|前渡歴史保存会|2024}}}} * {{Cite book |ja |editor=Akira Kimura |title=Visits to Major Shrines of Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{ill|lt=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060|WD=Q3335060|1=Nagara Tenjin Shrine}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|木村|1997}}}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Tejikarao Shrine (Kakamigahara)|Q11496922|WD=Q11496922|1=Tejikaraō Shrine (Kakamigahara)}} * {{ill|lt=Murakuni Shrine|Q11523055|WD=Q11523055|1=Murakuni Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Murakuni Masumida Shrine|Q11523053|WD=Q11523053|1=Murakuni Masumida Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Kasami Shrine|Q11398477|WD=Q11398477|1=Kasami Shrine}} === External links === * {{Instagram|miijinja|Mii Shrine}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mii Shrine (Kakamigahara)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mii Shrine (Kakamigahara)]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kakamigahara, Gifu]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gifu Prefecture]]

(Kakamigahara)}} [[JA:御井神社 (各務原市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11488771}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Mii Shrine | image = [[File:Mii jinja Kakamigahara2008-2.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|qid=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kakamigahara|Q641491|qid=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}} Mii-cho 5-8 | deity = {{ill|lt=Kimata-no-kami|Q11092833|qid=Q11092833|lt=Mii-no-kami|1=Kimata-no-Kami}} | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|23|23.35|N|136|50|15.33|E|display=title,inline}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (small)
{{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|qid=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = Third Saturday/Sunday of October | map = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|lat_deg=35|lat_min=23|lat_sec=23.3|lon_deg=136|lon_min=50|lon_sec=15.3|label=Mii Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|23|23.35|N|136|50|15.33|E}}}} }} '''Mii Shrine''' (みいじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara|Q641491|qid=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|qid=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}} (former Kakamigun, Mino Province). === Overview === It is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. Mii-no-kami is worshiped as a deity of trees, water, and safe childbirth. The area is rich in groundwater, and Kakamigahara's water-source facilities are nearby. Historically, {{ill|lt=Mount Mii|Q11354265|qid=Q11354265|1=Mount Mii}} itself was considered part of the shrine precinct. Originally the local tutelary shrine of Mii Village (Saraki-mura), it is now regarded as the tutelary shrine of the Inaba area of Kakamigahara. It is one of the city's five {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|qid=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} shrines. === History === The founding date is unknown. Tradition says the original shrine stood on the south slope of Mt. Mii and later moved to the summit. In the Sengoku period, {{ill|lt=Mii Castle|Q11354255|qid=Q11354255|1=Miiyama Castle}} was built on the mountain by Toki-affiliated forces, and the shrine was moved to its current otabisho-area location at the foot of the mountain. A portion of the shrine was also divided and became Tenjin Shrine in Kaminakaya. Mii Castle fell in 1548 during conflict between the Toki and Saito sides and was abandoned. The current honden, noritoden, and haiden were completed in 1941.Shikinaikinpei Mii Jinja guide booklet. On December 18, 1952, it was designated a Kinpeisha by the Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines.{{sfn|木村|1997|p=73}} A ritual called ''sakaura shinji'' is held on the first Sunday of April: sake is poured into a bamboo segment with a small hole, sealed with paper, purified, and cast into a pond; if it sinks, the year is expected to be rainy and abundant, and if it floats, drought and poor harvest are expected. === Enshrined deities === === Main deity === * {{ill|lt=Kimata-no-kami|Q11092833|qid=Q11092833|lt=Mii-no-kami|1=Kimata-no-Kami}} === Goshi (merged deities) === * {{ill|qid=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi|Q478504|qid=Q478504|qid=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-kami|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-kami|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} === Subsidiary shrines === * Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}) * Mikuwa Shrine ({{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|qid=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}}) * Akiba Shrine ({{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|qid=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Kagutsuchi-no-kami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}}) * Sumiyoshi Shrine ({{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|qid=Q1114013|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}) === Other monuments === * {{ill|lt=Chukonhi|Q11274518|qid=Q11274518|lt=Russo-Japanese War memorial|1=monument to the loyal dead}} (1909) * Statue of {{ill|lt=Nogi Maresuke|Q468122|qid=Q468122|1=Nogi Maresuke}} (1936) * Statue of {{ill|lt=Ninomiya Sontoku|Q968964|qid=Q968964|1=Ninomiya Sontoku}} === Major rituals === * Jan 1: Saitansai / New Year's festival * Feb 10: Mikuwa festival * Old lunar Feb 14: Akiba festival * Third Sunday of Feb: Kinen-sai * Mar 15: Sumiyoshi festival * First Saturday of Apr: Okunomiya festival and Suijin festival * First Sunday of Apr: Chinka-sai * Jun 30: Summer purification * Oct (second Sunday): Grand festival * Nov (fourth Sunday): Niiname-sai * Dec 30: Year-end purification * Dec 31: Joya festival * Monthly: 1st day and 15th day rites === Cultural properties === ; Kakamigahara City designated cultural properties * Komainu of Mii Shrine (one pair){{Cite web|ja|date=2024-08-20|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005178.html|title=City designated cultural properties|publisher=Kakamigahara City|accessdate=2013-04-25}} * Archaeological materials excavated from Mii Kofun (3 items) === Location === * {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|qid=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kakamigahara|Q641491|qid=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}}{{ill|lt=Mii-cho (Kakamigahara)|Q131870038|qid=Q131870038|lt=Mii-cho|1=Mii-cho}} 5-8 === Transport === * Kakamigahara Fureai Bus (Inaba line), get off at "Mii-cho Kominkan-mae," about 3 minutes on foot. === Okunomiya === [[File:Mii Shrine at Mount Mii 2010-12-19.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Okunomiya of Mii Shrine near the summit of Mt. Mii]] Near the summit of Mt. Mii, where the former shrine once stood. It was divided from Mii Shrine after the fall of Mii Castle (date unknown). Mt. Mii also has ritual sites associated with iwakura worship and many excavated artifacts, as well as a kofun (Miiyama Kofun). === Deity === * Mii-no-kami === Address === * Government land without lot number, Naka, Kakamigahara (near summit of Mt. Mii) === Miiike Ryujin Shrine === A separate shrine of Mii Shrine, also called "Mii Shrine Ikenomiya" or "Miiike Ryujin Shrine." Mii Pond at the foot of Mt. Mii is said to have been dug by Mii-no-kami and is an oxbow-lake remnant of the old Kiso River channel. In 1929, during landfill work around Mii Pond, a worker operating an irrigation pump reportedly entered a trance and moved like a dragon crawling on the ground; after this and related experiences, a small shrine was established by enshrining the spirit of Mii Shrine together with a dragon deity.{{Sfn|前渡歴史保存会|2024|p=39}} The present building was reconstructed in 1976 using part of old materials. === Deity === * Dragon deity (Miiike Ryujin / Miike Ryujin) === Precinct shrines === * Suijin * Kappa shrine === Address === * {{ill|lt=Miiyamacho|Q133214736|qid=Q133214736|1=Miiyamacho}}, Kakamigahara (about 15 minutes on foot from Mii Shrine) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book |ja |editor=Maedo History Preservation Society |title=Shrines and Temples: Kakamigahara Inaba District Edition |publisher=Maedo History Preservation Society |date=2024 |ref={{SfnRef|前渡歴史保存会|2024}}}} * {{Cite book |ja |editor=Akira Kimura |title=Visits to Major Shrines of Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{ill|lt=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060|qid=Q3335060|1=Nagara Tenjin Shrine}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|木村|1997}}}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Tejikarao Shrine (Kakamigahara)|Q11496922|qid=Q11496922|1=Tejikaraō Shrine (Kakamigahara)}} * {{ill|lt=Murakuni Shrine|Q11523055|qid=Q11523055|1=Murakuni Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Murakuni Masumida Shrine|Q11523053|qid=Q11523053|1=Murakuni Masumida Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Kasami Shrine|Q11398477|qid=Q11398477|1=Kasami Shrine}} === External links === * {{Instagram|miijinja|Mii Shrine}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mii Shrine (Kakamigahara)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mii Shrine (Kakamigahara)]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kakamigahara, Gifu]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gifu Prefecture]]
